name: The Humming Seal
setup: A sealed stack door in the Archive's second gallery — no lock, no handle, only a hairline seam and a hum just below hearing. Pre-Transition work, or older.
hintTiers: The hum is not constant — it drops a half-tone whenever someone speaks nearby. It is listening.
The seam brightens faintly under any light that has passed through crystal. The door reads spectra, not keys.
The hum's rhythm matches the Water Keeper's old channel-gate cadence — a maintenance pattern. This is infrastructure, and infrastructure answers to its own language.

codexUnlocks.note: The contamination pattern you learned from Mara matches the seal's cadence — you could hum the sequence back.
The iridescent creeper's leaf-spacing repeats in the seam's etching — a planting diagram, and you know the order.

truth: GM-EYES-ONLY: The stack behind this seal holds pre-Transition watershed maintenance records — including the reclamation site's work orders. Solving it is a major water-crisis lead. The door was built to open for maintenance workers; it still thinks it has some.
stakes: Attempts cost focus and hours. The Archive Guardian watches every try with great interest.
